mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth in >80% of months but 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in >50% of those periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 3.0 percent signals a shift toward tighter monetary policy that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following a FEDFUNDS rise above 3.0%, SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of cases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production combined with robust retail sales growth indicates broad-based economic momentum that boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth, the 20-day SPX forward return is negative in at least 40% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime where UNRATE is below 5.5%, further declines in unemployment tighten the labor market and raise wage inflation fears, causing the S&P 500 to fall over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: While UNRATE is below 5.5%, if PAYEMS rises month-over-month and SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1,100 thousand units, it signals robust residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: After HOUST crosses above 1100, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 55% of cases over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the consumer price index erode real purchasing power and raise expectations of monetary tightening, depressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, 20-day SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in real GDP above the 12 trillion dollar level signals that the economy has reached a self-sustaining expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After GDPC1 breaks above 12000, 60-day SPX forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects rising factory output and capacity utilization, which boosts investor confidence in the economic outlook and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If INDPRO monthly growth rate is positive yet the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 55% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.
